title: Philippians 4:6-7 — 3 Shifts to Stop Spiraling
hook: Stop spiraling — 3 scripture-backed shifts to calm your mind now.
script: Your mind is racing, stomach tight, what if after what if. Philippians 4:6-7 says, Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God… and His peace will guard you. Shift one: Reframe worry as a cue, not a cage. When anxiety shows up, say, This is my reminder to pray. Shift two: Pray specifically, then add one thank you. Specifics calm the brain; gratitude widens perspective. Shift three: Redirect attention. Breathe in, The Lord is near. Exhale, I release this to You. Notice five things you see. Save this for when your thoughts race.

title: Midnight Anxiety? Try These 3 Biblical Shifts
hook: If you're feeling anxious, watch this for the next 30 seconds.
script: It’s 2 a.m. Your heart is loud. Your mind is listing everything you can’t control. Philippians 4:6-7 says to pray about everything, with thanksgiving, and God’s peace will guard your heart and mind in Christ. Shift one: Name it. Say, I feel anxious about ___. A named fear shrinks. Shift two: Pray it specifically, then add one thank-you from today. Gratitude cues safety. Shift three: Redirect. Touch something near, breathe slowly, and repeat, The Lord is near. In for four, out for six. Peace doesn’t mean the storm is gone; it means you’re not alone in it. Save this for tonight.

title: Release, Replace, Rest: Philippians 4 for Overthinking
hook: You may be overthinking this—try these 3 shifts from Philippians 4.
script: Anxiety is loud, but it’s not your leader. Philippians 4:6-7 invites a new rhythm. Shift one: Release. Open your hands and breathe out what-if thoughts, saying, God, I release this. Shift two: Replace. Trade rumination for request with thanksgiving: Lord, here’s exactly what I need, and thank You for this one good thing. Specifics focus the mind; gratitude signals safety. Shift three: Rest. Redirect attention to the present. Name three things you see and one way you’ll love someone today. Slow exhale activates calm, and God’s peace stands guard. Rewatch this as a 60-second reset whenever worry spikes.
